question_text: Let {𝑋𝑛}𝑛≥1 be a sequence of independent and identically distributed random variables having probability density function 𝑓(𝑥) = {𝑒−(𝑥−𝜃)⁡⁡⁡if⁡⁡𝑥≥𝜃 0⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡⁡otherwise, where 𝜃> 0. Consider the following statements: (I) 𝑛⁡∑ 𝑖=1

options.C: onverges in probability to 𝜃+1 2 , as 𝑛→∞. (II) ⁡ lim 𝑛→∞𝐸(min ⁡ {𝑋1, 𝑋2, … , 𝑋𝑛}) = 𝜃. Which of the above statements is/are true?
